title: Functions
summary: Write reusable code blocks using functions, understand parameters, return values, and scope.
content_html:
Functions are reusable blocks of code that perform a specific task. They reduce repetition and improve code organization.

Defining and Calling Functions

def greet(name):          # define
    return 'Hello, ' + name

result = greet('Alice')   # call
print(result)  # Hello, Alice

Parameters and Arguments

def add(a, b):            # a, b are parameters
    return a + b

add(3, 4)                 # 3, 4 are arguments

Default Parameters

def power(base, exp=2):
    return base ** exp

power(3)    # 9  (uses default exp=2)
power(2, 3) # 8

Multiple Return Values

def min_max(lst):
    return min(lst), max(lst)

lo, hi = min_max([3,1,4,1,5])

Scope

  • Local scope: variables inside a function only exist there
  • Global scope: variables outside functions are accessible everywhere
